name: Trail between Navajas and Jérica
name_original: Paseo por el interior del Alto Palancia — Circular desde Jérica
description: Hike the Vía Verde Ojos Negros between Navajas and Jérica, a 7.3-mile route with waterfalls, river views, and charming towns.
region_intro: Hiking around Jérica offers diverse natural landscapes shaped by the Palancia River, which carves dramatic cliffs and gorges. The region features mountainous terrain, including parts of the Calderona mountain range, providing panoramic viewpoints. Hikers can explore areas around the Regajo Reservoir and discover waterfalls like Salto de la Novia. The varied vegetation, influenced by a temperate climate, includes almond, peach, and cherry trees. Best hiking trails around Jérica The most popular hiking route is Trail between Navajas and Jérica, a 7.3 miles (11.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 19 minutes to complete. This moderate route connects two towns and offers scenic river views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Regajo Reservoir Loop, an easy 4.1 miles (6.7 km) path. This route provides views of the reservoir and passes by natural springs. Local hikers also love the Fuente de los Baños – Bride's Leap Waterfall loop from Riu Palància, a 6.5 miles (10.5 km) trail leading through river valleys and to the spectacular Salto de la Novia waterfall, often completed in about 2 hours 59 minutes. Hiking around Jérica is defined by the Palancia River, the Regajo Reservoir, and mountainous landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging routes. The routes in Jérica are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 150 reviews. More than 900 hikers have used komoot to explore Jérica's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Jérica?
A: Jérica offers a network of 15 hiking trails, catering to various skill levels. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars.

Q: Are there easy hiking options for beginners or families in Jérica?
A: Yes, Jérica features 4 easy trails perfect for beginners or families. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e879001284/regajo-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Regajo Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 6.7 km path offering views of the reservoir and natural springs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6911590" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jérica – Historic center of Jérica loop&lt;/a&gt; is another easy 4.1 km route that explores the town's charming center.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking in Jérica?
A: Jérica's hiking trails are rich with natural beauty. You can explore the dramatic cliffs and gorges carved by the Palancia River, especially in the area known as 'La Vuelta de la Hoz'. The region is also home to stunning waterfalls like the spectacular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1310997" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bride's Leap Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; (Salto de la Novia) and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2099448" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tío Juan Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6525088" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Montesinas Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic views of the surrounding landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Jérica?
A: Yes, Jérica has several circular routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e879001284/regajo-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Regajo Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular easy 6.7 km circular hike. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31468791" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de los Baños – Bride's Leap Waterfall loop from Riu Palància&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 10.5 km trail that takes you through river valleys and to the impressive Salto de la Novia waterfall.

Q: Can I hike to waterfalls in Jérica?
A: Absolutely! Jérica is known for its beautiful waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1310997" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bride's Leap Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; (Salto de la Novia) is a spectacular sight, and you can reach it via the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31468791" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de los Baños – Bride's Leap Waterfall loop from Riu Palància&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2099448" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tío Juan Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is another impressive natural attraction, especially after rainfall.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Jérica?
A: Thanks to its temperate climate, Jérica is suitable for hiking for much of the year. Spring is particularly beautiful with almond, peach, and cherry trees in bloom. Autumn also offers vibrant red, yellow, and brown hues in the vegetation. The Palancia River and its natural water wells provide opportunities for bathing during warmer months.

Q: Are there any trails that follow the Palancia River?
A: Yes, the Palancia River is a central feature of Jérica's landscape. The highly recommended 'La Vuelta de la Hoz' is a natural area with a well-signposted path along the river, featuring benches and picnic areas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e880271460/trail-between-navajas-and-jerica" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail between Navajas and Jérica&lt;/a&gt; also offers scenic river views as it connects the two towns.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Jérica's hiking trails?
A: Jérica's terrain is diverse, shaped by the Palancia River and its mountainous surroundings. You can expect paths along riverbanks, through gorges, and up into parts of the Calderona mountain range, offering panoramic viewpoints. The vegetation varies from almond and cherry groves to Mediterranean flora.

Q: Are there any historical sites or points of interest along the hiking routes?
A: Many hiking routes in Jérica can incorporate historical elements. You can explore the ruins of the castle, the San Roque chapel, and ancient walls within the town. Some routes, like the Circular Route to Randurias Fountain, even include remains of trenches, offering a glimpse into historical memory.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Jérica?
A: The hiking trails in Jérica are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 150 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse natural landscapes, the scenic river views, and the well-maintained paths that lead to beautiful waterfalls and reservoirs.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking options near Jérica?
A: For those seeking a longer challenge, Jérica is part of the GR 231 Ruta de Pinares a Sabinares - Comunidad Valenciana, a medium-difficulty trail that spans over 64 km, offering an extensive exploration of the region's diverse landscapes.
tips:
Very picturesque town in the interior of Alto Palancia with many points of interest to visit. You can visit the beautiful Mudejar Tower, the Castle and the Hermitage of San Roque among others. Also do a hiking route such as the Vuelta de la Hoz or follow the course of the Palancia River.
A very beautiful town that has it all and deserves to be visited!

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Trail between Navajas and Jérica?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily follows the Vía Verde de Ojos Negros, which is built on a disused railway line. This means the terrain is mostly flat and well-conditioned, with some sections even asphalted. It's suitable for walking, cycling, and even baby strollers, offering a smooth and accessible experience.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, this section of the Vía Verde de Ojos Negros is generally considered easy to moderate. It has minimal elevation gain, making it very suitable for families, children, and those new to hiking. The path is well-marked and easy to follow.

faqs.question: What natural attractions can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll be accompanied by the Palancia River, offering lush vegetation. Notable natural attractions include the impressive 60-meter Salto de la Novia waterfall and the Cascada del Tío Juan. The path also passes by the serene Regajo Reservoir. You'll also encounter tunnels, bridges, pine forests, and agricultural fields.

faqs.question: Are there any towns or historical sites to explore nearby?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail connects the charming towns of Navajas and Jérica. In Jérica, you can explore its medieval-style streets, bell tower, and castle. Navajas is also known for its abundance of natural springs and waterfalls.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the lush vegetation and water features, spring and autumn are ideal for comfortable temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be pleasant, especially with the river nearby, but it's always wise to check local weather conditions.

faqs.question: Is the Trail between Navajas and Jérica dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Vía Verde de Ojos Negros is generally accessible, specific regulations for dogs can vary. It's usually permitted to bring dogs on a leash, especially on greenways. Always ensure your dog is under control and clean up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access the trail in Alto Palancia?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail, which is part of the Vía Verde de Ojos Negros in the Alto Palancia region. It's freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Both Navajas and Jérica, as start/end points for the trail, offer parking options within the towns or near the Vía Verde access points. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival for the most convenient parking areas.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is primarily part of the Entre Navajas y Jérica (81%) and also intersects with sections of the Nacimiento del Palancia (40%), Vía Verde Ojos Negros (11%), La Conquista de Valencia (10%), Camino de la Muela (17%), and Calle Santa Ana (12%). It also forms part of significant national routes such as the Ruta del Grial and the Camino del Cid.

faqs.question: Are there opportunities to refill water bottles along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the area is known for its natural springs. You'll find various natural springs along the route, such as the Fuente del Baño, where you can refill your water bottles.

faqs.question: Is it possible to do a loop hike, or is it primarily a point-to-point route?
faqs.answer: The core Navajas-Jérica section of the Vía Verde is typically a point-to-point route. However, there are local loops around Navajas that can extend your hike, though some of these might be considered more moderate due to increased distance and elevation gain compared to the main Vía Verde section.
